natural_language_question: How many tracks are in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_tracks FROM tracks

natural_language_question: How many unique genres are there?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS genre_count FROM genres

natural_language_question: How many unique artists are in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS artist_count FROM artists

natural_language_question: What is the average popularity score across all tracks?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(popularity), 2) AS avg_popularity FROM tracks

natural_language_question: What is the average tempo across all tracks?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(tempo), 2) AS avg_tempo FROM tracks

natural_language_question: How many tracks have explicit lyrics?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS explicit_track_count FROM tracks WHERE explicit = true

natural_language_question: What are the 10 most popular songs in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT track_name, album_name, popularity FROM v_track_details ORDER BY popularity DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: What is the longest track in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT track_name, album_name, duration_min FROM v_track_details ORDER BY duration_min DESC LIMIT 1

natural_language_question: What is the minimum, maximum, and average danceability score?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(MIN(danceability), 3) AS min_danceability, ROUND(MAX(danceability), 3) AS max_danceability, ROUND(AVG(danceability), 3) AS avg_danceability FROM tracks

natural_language_question: Show me all acoustic tracks with popularity above 80
expected_sql: SELECT track_name, album_name, popularity, acousticness FROM v_track_details WHERE acousticness > 0.8 AND popularity > 80 ORDER BY popularity DESC LIMIT 20

natural_language_question: What are the top 10 genres by number of tracks?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY track_count DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: What is the average danceability score by genre, ranked highest to lowest?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(danceability), 3) AS avg_danceability FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY avg_danceability DESC

natural_language_question: Which genres have the highest average energy?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(energy), 3) AS avg_energy FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY avg_energy DESC LIMIT 15

natural_language_question: Which artists have the most tracks in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_artist_tracks GROUP BY artist_name ORDER BY track_count DESC LIMIT 15

natural_language_question: What is the average song duration in minutes by genre?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(duration_min), 2) AS avg_duration_min FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY avg_duration_min DESC

natural_language_question: Which genres have songs that are both highly danceable and highly energetic?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, COUNT(*) AS track_count, ROUND(AVG(popularity), 1) AS avg_popularity FROM v_track_details WHERE danceability > 0.7 AND energy > 0.7 GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY track_count DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: Classify tracks as short (under 3 min), medium (3-5 min), or long (over 5 min) and count each group
expected_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN duration_min < 3 THEN 'Short (<3 min)' WHEN duration_min <= 5 THEN 'Medium (3-5 min)' ELSE 'Long (>5 min)' END AS duration_category, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY duration_category ORDER BY MIN(duration_min)

natural_language_question: Which artists have the most tracks in the pop genre?
expected_sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_artist_tracks WHERE genre_name = 'pop' GROUP BY artist_name ORDER BY track_count DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: What are the happiest genres based on average valence score?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(valence), 3) AS avg_valence, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY avg_valence DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: What percentage of tracks in each genre are explicit?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN explicit THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS explicit_count, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN explicit THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS explicit_pct FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY explicit_pct DESC LIMIT 15

natural_language_question: Show the relationship between loudness and energy by genre
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(loudness), 2) AS avg_loudness, ROUND(AVG(energy), 3) AS avg_energy, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY avg_energy DESC

natural_language_question: Which artists have the highest average popularity? (with at least 5 tracks)
expected_sql: SELECT artist_name, ROUND(AVG(popularity), 1) AS avg_popularity, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_artist_tracks GROUP BY artist_name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 ORDER BY avg_popularity DESC LIMIT 15

natural_language_question: Which artists have average popularity above the overall dataset average?
expected_sql: SELECT artist_name, ROUND(AVG(popularity), 1) AS avg_popularity, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_artist_tracks GROUP BY artist_name HAVING AVG(popularity) > (SELECT AVG(popularity) FROM tracks) AND COUNT(*) >= 3 ORDER BY avg_popularity DESC LIMIT 20

natural_language_question: Within each genre, what are the top 3 most popular tracks?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, track_name, popularity, rank_in_genre FROM (SELECT genre_name, track_name, popularity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY genre_name ORDER BY popularity DESC) AS rank_in_genre FROM v_track_details) ranked WHERE rank_in_genre <= 3 ORDER BY genre_name, rank_in_genre

natural_language_question: Which genres have above-average popularity AND above-average danceability?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(popularity), 1) AS avg_popularity, ROUND(AVG(danceability), 3) AS avg_danceability, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name HAVING AVG(popularity) > (SELECT AVG(popularity) FROM tracks) AND AVG(danceability) > (SELECT AVG(danceability) FROM tracks) ORDER BY avg_popularity DESC

natural_language_question: How does average popularity change with the musical mode (major vs minor) across genres?
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, mode_name, ROUND(AVG(popularity), 1) AS avg_popularity, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name, mode_name ORDER BY genre_name, mode_name

natural_language_question: What is the correlation between acousticness and energy by genre? Show genres where acoustic tracks are much less energetic.
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN acousticness > 0.5 THEN energy END), 3) AS avg_energy_acoustic, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN acousticness <= 0.5 THEN energy END), 3) AS avg_energy_non_acoustic, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 50 ORDER BY (avg_energy_non_acoustic - avg_energy_acoustic) DESC LIMIT 10

natural_language_question: Which musical keys are associated with the highest average valence (happiness)?
expected_sql: SELECT kn.key_name, ROUND(AVG(t.valence), 3) AS avg_valence, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM tracks t JOIN key_names kn ON t.key = kn.key GROUP BY kn.key_name ORDER BY avg_valence DESC

natural_language_question: Find artists who appear in more than 3 different genres
expected_sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT genre_name) AS genre_count, STRING_AGG(DISTINCT genre_name, ', ' ORDER BY genre_name) AS genres FROM v_artist_tracks GROUP BY artist_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT genre_name) > 3 ORDER BY genre_count DESC LIMIT 15

natural_language_question: What is the audio fingerprint of the most popular genre? Show average values for all audio features.
expected_sql: SELECT genre_name, ROUND(AVG(danceability), 3) AS avg_danceability, ROUND(AVG(energy), 3) AS avg_energy, ROUND(AVG(valence), 3) AS avg_valence, ROUND(AVG(acousticness), 3) AS avg_acousticness, ROUND(AVG(instrumentalness), 3) AS avg_instrumentalness, ROUND(AVG(speechiness), 3) AS avg_speechiness, ROUND(AVG(tempo), 1) AS avg_tempo, ROUND(AVG(loudness), 2) AS avg_loudness, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM v_track_details GROUP BY genre_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
